# WASM Compiler Comparison Notes (2026-03-19)

## Compiler Comparison (add + factorial)

| Compiler | WASM Size | Functions | Instructions | ABAP Lines |
|----------|----------:|----------:|-------------:|-----------:|
| AssemblyScript | 97 B | 2 | 18 | 89 |
| porffor (TS) | 406 B | 4 | 117 | 115 |
| porffor (JS) | 29 KB | 106 | 10,641 | 2,569 |
| ABAP tokenizer (porffor) | 55 KB | 109 | 21,804 | 4,684 |
| Javy/QuickJS (eval) | 1.2 MB | 1,410 | 453,473 | 107,337 |
| abaplint (Javy) | 26.5 MB | 1,410 | 453,473 | 396,518 |

## Correctness Verification

- **Javy/QuickJS**: ✅ Verified — factorial(10)=3628800, correct output
- **porffor**: ⚠️ Experimental (v0.61) — compiles but returns 0 for all tests.
  Recursion may not work. The WASM validates in Node.js but runtime
  results are incorrect. Not production-ready for complex code.
- **AssemblyScript**: ✅ Compiles correctly, smallest output.
  Requires TypeScript-like source with explicit i32/i64 types.
  NOT compatible with standard TypeScript (different type system).

## Recommendations

1. **For production**: Javy/QuickJS — works, proven, stable
2. **For small utilities**: AssemblyScript — tiny output, but requires rewrite
3. **For future**: porffor — watch for maturity (v1.0+), then re-evaluate
4. **Best hybrid**: QuickJS-as-ABAP (already compiled) + load JS at runtime
